content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacmmcnto, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Shirley Concolino 
City Clerk Sacramento 
915 I Street, New City Hall 5th Floor 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-13-119 
Dear Ms. Concolino: 
September 12, 2013 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").
1 
QUESTIONS 
I. Whether Section 84203(b) and 84204 of the Act can be modified to include email as a 
method of delivery and exclude facsimile transmission? 
2. Whether the Statement of Organization Form 410 copy can be modified to include email 
as a method of delivery and exclude facsimile transmission? 
CONCLUSIONS 
I. Section 84203(b) and 84204 of the Act may be modified to include email as a method of 
de) ivery for late contribution and late independent expenditures reports. However. it may 
not be modified to exclude facsimile transmission. 
2. The Statement of Organization Form 410 copy may also be modified to include email as 
a method of delivery, but facsimile transmission may not be excluded. 
1 The: Political Reform Act 11, contained in GO\crnment Code: Sc:ctions 81000 through910 I-+. All statutory 
rdcrcnccs arc: to the Government Cnde, unle1,s otherwise indicated. The regulations of the: Fair Political Practices 
Commission arc contained m Secuom, 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the: California Code nf Regulation<;. All 
regulatory references arc 10 Tnh! 2. Division 6 of the California Cot.le of Regulations. unless otherwise indica1cd. 


FACTS 
File No. A-13-119 
Page No. 2 
The City of Sacramento is dedicated to reducing it!:. carbon footprint, and creating 
paperless processes whenever possible. As pan of this effort, the City Clerk is interested in 
keeping as many filings submitted electronic as possible. This will help reduce the number of 
filings received. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 84203(b) and Section 84204 state that a late contribution or late independent 
expenditure shall be reported by facsimile transmission, guaranteed overnight delivery, or 
personal delivery within 24 hours of the time it is made. 
Where a statute or regulation provides that a report or statement can be faxed, the agency 
can also permit the reports or statements to be sent by email where the agency has set up a 
procedure to accept them by email. However, facsimile trani;mi-;sion may not be excluded as a 
method of delivery since the statute explicitly permits i.uch method. 
The Statement of Organization Form 410 states that filings must be made by fax, 
guaranteed overnight delivery, personal delivery, or online (if onlinc filing is available). 
Form 410 copy may also be modified to include email as a method of delivery as long as 
the agency receiving the Form has arranged to accept them by email. But facsimile transmission 
may not be excluded since the Form explicitly allows fax as a method of filing. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HGK:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
-tt1 
By: 
Heidi G. Kim 
Legal Intern, Legal Division
